annotations.label: Law of sines

anchor.snippet: the law of sines (sometimes called the sine formula or sine rule ) is a mathematical equation relating the lengths of the sides of any triangle to the sines of its angles

annotations.note: `law_sin` (alias of `sin_angle_mul_dist_eq_sin_angle_mul_dist`) gives `sin(∠ p₁ p₂ p₃) * dist p₂ p₃ = sin(∠ p₃ p₁ p₂) * dist p₃ p₁`, the angle-at-point form of the law of sines.

annotations.label: Altitude equality giving law of sines

anchor.snippet: the triangle's altitude can be computed as b sin γ or as c sin β

annotations.note: Mathlib does not have a dedicated lemma expressing a triangle's altitude as `b sin γ = c sin β`; the law of sines is proved by other means in `Triangle.lean`.

annotations.label: Ambiguous case conditions

anchor.snippet: the following conditions would need to be fulfilled for the case to be ambiguous

annotations.note: No definition or lemma in Mathlib about the SSA ambiguous case of triangle solution.

annotations.label: Both triangles valid in ambiguous case

anchor.snippet: each of angles β and β′ produces a valid triangle

annotations.note: No statement in Mathlib that the SSA ambiguous case yields two valid triangles.

annotations.label: Common ratio equals circumdiameter

anchor.snippet: the common value of the three fractions is actually the diameter of the triangle's circumcircle

annotations.note: Mathlib defines `Simplex.circumsphere`/`circumradius` but does not state that `a / sin α = 2R` for a triangle.

annotations.note: The Thales/inscribed-angle derivation of the law of sines via the circumcircle is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Tetrahedron polar sine ratio

anchor.snippet: the absolute value of the polar sine ( psin ) of the normal vectors to the three facets that share a vertex of the tetrahedron, divided by the area of the fourth facet will not depend upon the choice of the vertex

annotations.note: Mathlib has no polar-sine notion (`polarSin`/`psin`) nor any tetrahedron law-of-sines statement.

annotations.label: n-dimensional simplex law of sines

anchor.snippet: for an n -dimensional simplex

annotations.note: No higher-dimensional simplex generalization of the law of sines is formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Al-Tusi's planar law of sines

anchor.snippet: In any plane triangle, the ratio of the sides is equal to the ratio of the sines of the angles opposite to those sides

annotations.note: The ratio form `sin(∠ p₁ p₂ p₃) / dist p₃ p₁ = sin(∠ p₃ p₁ p₂) / dist p₂ p₃` is exactly al-Tusi's stated proportion of sines to opposite sides.
